quizzes.title: Tech & Programming Trivia
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge on computer science, coding, and tech history!

questions.text: Who is considered the father of computers?
questions.explanation: Charles Babbage designed the Analytical Engine, which is considered the first general mechanical computer concept.

questions.text: What does CSS stand for in web design?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets, used to describe the presentation of a document written in HTML.

questions.text: Which programming language was originally named 'Oak'?
questions.explanation: Java was originally initiated by James Gosling and named 'Oak' after an oak tree that stood outside his office.

questions.text: What is the primary protocol used to secure communication over the web?
questions.explanation: HTTPS (Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure) encrypts communication using TLS/SSL to protect data integrity and privacy.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a relational database system?
questions.explanation: MongoDB is a document-oriented, non-relational (NoSQL) database, whereas MySQL, PostgreSQL, and Oracle are relational (SQL) databases.

quizzes.title: World Geography Challenge
quizzes.description: Embark on a journey to identify countries, capitals, and natural wonders!

questions.text: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital city of Australia, selected as a compromise between rival cities Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.text: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions, covering about 30% of the Earth's surface.

questions.text: Which country is home to the landmark 'Machu Picchu'?
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu is a 15th-century Inca citadel located in the Eastern Cordillera of southern Peru.

questions.text: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: While there is ongoing debate, the Nile River is traditionally considered the longest river in the world, stretching over 6,650 kilometers.

questions.text: Which European nation features the active volcano Mount Etna?
questions.explanation: Mount Etna is an active stratovolcano on the east coast of Sicily, Italy.

quizzes.title: Cosmic Science Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your grasp on astronomy, physics, and the mysteries of the universe!

questions.text: Approximately how long does it take light from the Sun to reach Earth?
questions.explanation: Light travels at 300,000 km/s. Given the Sun's distance from Earth, it takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds for sunlight to reach us.

questions.text: Which particle in an atom carries a negative electrical charge?
questions.explanation: Electrons carry a negative charge (-1e) and orbit the atomic nucleus, which contains protons (positive) and neutrons (neutral).

questions.text: What is the main element that makes up the Sun?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen makes up about 73% of the Sun's mass, with helium constituting most of the remaining 25%.

questions.text: Who formulated the laws of electromagnetic induction?
questions.explanation: Michael Faraday discovered electromagnetic induction in 1831, showing that a magnetic field can induce an electric current.

questions.text: What is the escape velocity required to break free from Earth's gravity?
questions.explanation: The escape velocity of Earth is approximately 11.2 kilometers per second (about 40,320 km/h or 25,050 mph).
